title: Node HTTP Primitives, Connections, Protocols, Timeouts, and Shutdown · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for node http primitives, connections, protocols, timeouts, and shutdown and demands the smallest safe Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace IncomingMessage and ServerResponse as streams with header commitment, body ownership, backpressure, abort, error, and close lifetimes.
Configure requestTimeout, headersTimeout, keepAliveTimeout, keepAliveTimeoutBuffer, maxHeadersCount, and maximum header size from a threat and latency budget.
Distinguish HTTP/1.1, Node HTTP/2 compatibility behavior, ALPN, TLS, upgrades, CONNECT, and the explicit external boundary for HTTP/3.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Protocol Transfer Evidence
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 7712 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate protocol support with explicit header, request, and drain policy; retain a screen-reader-readable decision trace; then change the tenant and prove denial before repository access. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Require live TLS identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Protocol Transfer Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require live tls identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models. For Node and Express server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Protocol Transfer Evidence, keep Protocol Transfer Evidence connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
